    if not gdb:
        die("No working riscv64-elf-gdb found (needed for FPGA board runs)")
    ok(f"GDB: {gdb}")

    # Compile with FPGA linker script
    src = os.path.join(tmpdir, "hello_fpga.c")
    elf = os.path.join(tmpdir, "hello_fpga.adx")
    with open(src, "w") as f:
        f.write('#include <stdio.h>\nint main(void) { printf("HELLO_FPGA_OK\\n"); return 0; }\n')

    cmd = [gcc, "-O2", "-o", elf, src,
           "-mtune=andes-45-series", "-mext-vector=zve64d", "-mvh"]
    if os.path.isfile(ld_script):
        cmd.append(f"-T{ld_script}")
    cmd.append("-lm")

    r = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if r.returncode != 0:
        die(f"FPGA compilation failed:\n{r.stderr}")
    ok("Compiled for FPGA")

    # Remove First_Reset_done so wrapper resets the board
    first_reset = os.path.join(LIBNN_ROOT, "project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done")
    if os.path.isfile(first_reset):
        os.remove(first_reset)

    env = os.environ.copy()
    env["MYPC"] = host
    env["MYPORT"] = str(port)
    env["PATH"] = f"{bin_dir}:{env.get('PATH', '')}"

    # The expect script hardcodes riscv64-elf-gdb-nopython.
    # If missing, create a symlink in tmpdir and prepend to PATH.
    gdb_nopy = os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb-nopython")
    if not os.path.isfile(gdb_nopy):
        link = os.path.join(tmpdir, f"{prefix}gdb-nopython")
        os.symlink(gdb, link)
        env["PATH"] = f"{tmpdir}:{env['PATH']}"
        ok(f"Symlinked {prefix}gdb-nopython -> {os.path.basename(gdb)}")

    ok(f"Running on FPGA ({host}:{port}, timeout=120s) ...")
    try:
        r = subprocess.run(
            [sim_wrapper, elf],
            capture_output=True, text=True,
            timeout=120, env=env,
            cwd=os.path.join(LIBNN_ROOT, "project/ax45mpv"),
        )
    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
        die("FPGA run timed out (120s)")

    combined = (r.stdout or "") + (r.stderr or "")
    if "HELLO_FPGA_OK" in combined:
        ok("FPGA output: HELLO_FPGA_OK")
        return

#!/bin/bash
#set -x

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# This file is a board simulator wrapper shell script for both nds32le-elf & nds32be-elf,
# which is called during the gcc/plumhall/supertest testsuite verification flow.
#
# Currently, the nds32 simulator -- run binary on board -- does not run program in intuitive way.
# The simulatior requires a gdb expect file in which the execution file is strictly specified.
# We use this wrapper script to complete the work so that the testsuite can easily use it to do simulation.

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# check whether MYPC environment variable is set up
if [ -z ${MYPC} ]; then
    echo
    echo "Warning! MYPC environment variable (where ICEman launches on) is not set yet."
    echo "GDB will take localhost as the target remote."
    echo
fi

# check whether MYPORT environment variable is set up
if [ -z ${MYPORT} ]; then
    echo
    echo "Error! MYPORT environment variable is not set yet."
    echo "Please set MYPORT to the socket port number of ICEman for GDB connection."
    echo
    exit 1
fi

# the following variables will combine directory name and file name to form a full-path
RUN_GDB=$SIMULATOR_DIR/gdb-ice-riscv64.exp

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# set gcc testsuite should be "yes" or "no", it will effect how to get return value.
GCC="no"

# set the board cache will be "on" or "lm". lm measns using local memory.
CACHE="on"

# set the board need reset-and-run or not.
RESET_BOARD="off"

# setup the PMA for HVM (it could be "on" or "off")
HVM_PMA="off"

# setup the DDR latency (it could be "on" or "off")
DDR_LATENCY="on"

# enable the non-blocking load (it could be "on" or "off")
NON_BLOCKING_LOAD="on"

# setup the D$ write-around threshold (it could be 0~3)
DC_WA_THRESHOLD="1"

# we should reset the board when we run first test case in every testsuite.
if [ ! -f "$SIMULATOR_DIR/First_Reset_done" ]; then
    RESET="off"
    touch $SIMULATOR_DIR/First_Reset_done
else
    RESET=$RESET_BOARD
fi

# physically invoke simulator
if test -r CVALSTDIN
then
	expect $RUN_GDB $RUN_FILE $GCC $CACHE $RESET $HVM_PMA $DDR_LATENCY $NON_BLOCKING_LOAD $DC_WA_THRESHOLD $CVALSTDIN
else
	expect $RUN_GDB $RUN_FILE $GCC $CACHE $RESET $HVM_PMA $DDR_LATENCY $NON_BLOCKING_LOAD $DC_WA_THRESHOLD
fi
# Note. Please do NOT add any command between expect $RUN_GDB blah blah blah... and ret=$? to make
# sure the scirpt can get correct return value from GDB.
ret=$?

cd ${OLD_PWD}

exit ${ret}

#!/usr/bin/expect

# Set some variables

# Setup the timeout value while simulating each test case on real board.
# This value should be replaced by build system or manually changed.
#   e.g. "set timeout 500"
set timeout 600

# Setup the nds32 gdb full-path so that we can execute it to connect to aice.
# This value should be replaced by build system or manually changed.
#   e.g. "set gdb /path/to/nds32le-elf-newlib-v3/bin/nds32le-elf-gdb"
set gdb riscv64-elf-gdb-nopython

# Setup the host machine that iceman is running on.
# This value should be replaced by build system or manually changed.
#   e.g. "set host apc188"
set host $::env(MYPC)

#Setup the port opened by iceman connecting to real board.
# This value should be replaced by build system or manually changed.
#   e.g. "set port 1234"
set port $::env(MYPORT)

send "set pagination off\n"

set elf [lindex $argv 0]
set gcc [lindex $argv 1]
set cache [lindex $argv 2]
set reset [lindex $argv 3]
set hvm_pma [lindex $argv 4]
set ddr_latency [lindex $argv 5]
set non_blocking_load [lindex $argv 6]
set dc_wa_threshold [lindex $argv 7]
set stdin [lindex $argv 8]
set gdb_prompt_pattern ".*gdb\[)] "

set fail_ret 1

# Execute binary
if { $gcc == "yes" } {
  spawn $gdb -e "$elf"
} else {
  spawn $gdb -e "$elf" -return-child-result -q
}

# When timeout, will send contrlC to stop this program
proc ctrlC {} {
	send \003
	set status 123

set status 123
	send "quit\n"
	expect {
		"Quit anyway?" {
			send "y\n"
		}
	}
	expect eof
	exit $status
}

proc cache_on {} {
    send_user "Start to setup L2C.\n"
	l2c_on
	send_user "Setup L2C done.\n"

    send_user "Start to enable I/D cache, I/D cache prefetch and D cache coherence.\n"
	enable_cache "603"
	expect gdb { send_user "Enable I/D cache, I/D cache prefetch  and D cache coherence done.\n" }
}

proc l2c_on {} {
    #addr of SMU.SYSTEMCFG
    set smu_system_cfg_addr [format %x [expr {0xF0100008} ] ]
    set smu_system_cfg_val [ get_mem_value "$smu_system_cfg_addr" ]

    #SMU.SYSTEMCFG.L2C
    set l2c_ctl_offset 7
    set l2c_ctl_mask [format %x [expr {0x100} ] ]

    set is_l2c_supported [format %d [expr ($smu_system_cfg_val & 0x$l2c_ctl_mask) ] ]
    if {$is_l2c_supported} {
        send_user "CPU supports L2C cache.\n"

        # addr of L2C configuration register
        set l2c_cfg_addr [format %x [expr {0xE0500000} ] ]
        set l2c_cfg_val [ get_mem_value "$l2c_cfg_addr" ]
        set l2c_ver_off 24
        set l2c_ver_mask [expr 0xff << $l2c_ver_off]
        set l2c_ctl_addr [format %x [expr {0xE0500008} ] ]

1	---
2	name: libnn-optimizer
3	description: |
4	  Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
5	  in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
6	  libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
7	  optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
8	  or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
9	  "libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
10	  "phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
11	  opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
12	argument-hint: "[validate|gather|baseline|iterate|status|reset] [function_name]"
13	allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Edit, Write, Agent
14	---
15	
16	# libnn Optimizer
17	
18	Autonomous optimization of libnn NN operator functions targeting RISC-V FPGA hardware.
19	Five-phase FSM: gather -> baseline -> iterate -> evaluate -> done.
20	State persisted in `opt/state.json`.
21	
22	## Commands
23	
24	- `/libnn-optimizer validate` - Run config validation (Step 0)
25	- `/libnn-optimizer gather <function_perf_test>` - Start Phase 1: parse task spec + target features
26	- `/libnn-optimizer baseline` - Run Phase 2: measure unmodified library
27	- `/libnn-optimizer iterate` - Run Phase 3: one optimization iteration

---
name: libnn-optimizer
description: |
  Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
  in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
  libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
  optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
  or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
  "libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
  "phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
  opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
argument-hint: "[validate|gather|baseline|iterate|status|reset] [function_name]"
allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Edit, Write, Agent
---

# libnn Optimizer

Autonomous optimization of libnn NN operator functions targeting RISC-V FPGA hardware.
Five-phase FSM: gather -> baseline -> iterate -> evaluate -> done.
State persisted in `opt/state.json`.

## Commands

- `/libnn-optimizer validate` - Run config validation (Step 0)
- `/libnn-optimizer gather <function_perf_test>` - Start Phase 1: parse task spec + target features
- `/libnn-optimizer baseline` - Run Phase 2: measure unmodified library
- `/libnn-optimizer iterate` - Run Phase 3: one optimization iteration
- `/libnn-optimizer status` - Show current state from opt/state.json
- `/libnn-optimizer reset` - Reset state to initial

Arguments received: `$ARGUMENTS`

## Project Paths

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/config.toml` | Target config (user-owned, NEVER modify) |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/state.json` | Optimization state (LLM reads/writes) |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/script/` | Validation and helper scripts |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/fpga_config/` | FPGA Verilog .inc files |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/patches/` | Per-iteration diffs (audit trail) |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/build/` | Build artifacts |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/log/` | Build/test/perf logs |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/plan.md` | Full implementation plan |
| `/home/nick/work/libnn/` | libnn source tree |

## Step 0: Validate Configuration

Run before any optimization work. Validates config.toml and all referenced resources.

```bash
python3 /home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/script/validate_config.py --strace
```

Seven sequential checks, fail-fast:

| Step | Check                                                               | On Failure                                   |
|------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------|
| 1    | Load config.toml - all required sections/keys                       | config.toml                                  |
| 2    | Toolchain - bin_dir exists, gcc with Andes flags                    | Script suggests alternatives found on system |
| 3    | Compile hello world using the above toolchain - RISC-V ELF produced | Toolchain issue                              |
| 4    | QEMU - binary exists, runs hello.adx via strace semihosting         | Check QEMU path                              |
| 5    | FPGA .inc - parse hardware features (VLEN, cache, ISA)              | Check fpga_config path                       |
| 6    | FPGA connectivity - TCP to board host:port                          | Network/board issue                          |
| 7    | FPGA run - compile + run hello on board via GDB                     | Board/GDB issue                              |

If Step 2 fails, the script auto-discovers alternative toolchain paths. Report them to the user. Do NOT modify config.toml.

If QEMU or FPGA steps fail, report the error and ask the user which steps to skip for offline development.

## Phase 1: Gather

Parse task spec and target features. No builds.

1. **Read config.toml.** Extract toolchain PATH, QEMU binary, FPGA host:port, CPU, VLEN.

2. **Read FPGA .inc file** (path from `[target].fpga_config`, relative to `opt/`). Extract:
   - Vector: `NDS_VLEN`, `NDS_DLEN`, `NDS_ELEN`
   - D-cache: `NDS_DCACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_DCACHE_WAY`
   - I-cache: `NDS_ICACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_CACHE_LINE_SIZE`
   - L2: `NDS_L2C_CACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_L2C_WAY`, `NDS_L2_DATA_WIDTH`
   - ISA: `NDS_RVV_SUPPORT`, `NDS_VECTOR_VDOT_SUPPORT`, `NDS_INT4_VECTOR_LOAD_SUPPORT`, `NDS_BFLOAT16_SUPPORT`, `NDS_QMAC_SUPPORT`
   - Bus: `NDS_BIU_DATA_WIDTH`

   Parse using regex: `` `define\s+<KEY>\s+(\d+) `` for integers, `` `define\s+<KEY>_INT\s+(\d+) `` or `` `define\s+<KEY>\s+"(yes|no)" `` for booleans.

3. **Read source_input** (perf test or model C file):
   - **Single mode:** parse `#define` block from `Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_<func>.c`. Extract shapes (INPUT_X, INPUT_Y, INPUT_CH, KERNEL_X, etc.), quantization params (OUT_SHIFT, OUT_MULT, offsets, activation min/max), buffer sizes. Resolve function name from `riscv_nn_*()` call site.
   - **Model mode:** parse model `.c` and `#include`d param headers. Build ordered `(function, shape-set)` list with call counts.

4. **Locate baseline source** at `/home/nick/work/libnn/Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c`. Record path.

5. **Derive optimization macro:** `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC_UPPER>` (single) or `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC_UPPER>_S<N>` (model).

6. **Write state.json.** Set `phase` to `"baseline"`.

**Verification:** state.json must contain:
- All shapes from the perf test `#define` block
- `target_features.vlen == 1024`
- `target_features.dcache_kb == 32`
- `target_features.l2_kb == 8192`
- Correct `source_file` path under `Source/`

## Phase 2: Baseline

Measure unmodified library on target.

1. Rebuild baseline:
   ```bash
   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/build.sh <cpu>
   ```

2. QEMU correctness (strace mode for semihosting fd 9):
   ```bash
   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh <test> --strace --vlen <vlen>
   ```
   Accept only "accuracy checking pass". Failure aborts -- baseline is broken.

3. FPGA perf measurement:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf && cd /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf
   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/test_perf.sh ax45mpv riscv64-elf-gcc BOARD <function>
   ```
   Parse `performance.csv` for cycles and instructions.

4. Write `baseline` to state.json. Initialize empty `history`. Set `phase` to `"iterate"`.

## Phase 3: Iterate

One iteration = one candidate optimization. Loop until `max_iterations` or no improvement >= `min_improvement_pct` for 2 consecutive attempts.

Per iteration:

1. **Plan** - Inspect current best source (or baseline on iter 1). Pick one concrete change. Record in `notes`.

2. **Edit** - Modify `Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c`: add optimized path under `#ifdef ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>`. Replace prior iteration's block if present. Baseline `#else` stays byte-for-byte intact.

3. **Build** - Rebuild `libnn.a` twice:
   - Without macro (sanity -- baseline still compiles)
   - With `-DENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>` for perf

4. **Correctness** - QEMU strace test. Fail -> `accepted=false`, skip perf.

5. **Perf** - `test_perf.sh`. Collect cycles/instructions.

6. **Commit** - Git commit in libnn tree. Snapshot diff to `opt/patches/iter_<N>_<func>.patch`.

7. **Accept/reject** - `accepted = pass && cycles < best.cycles`. Update `best` if accepted.

8. Append to `history`. Persist state.json.

## Phase 4: Evaluate

1. Restore `best.commit` in libnn worktree.
2. Rerun full QEMU suite for touched functions.
3. Rerun FPGA perf, confirm within +/- 0.5% of `best.cycles`.
4. Model mode only: re-profile all (function, shape-set) pairs.
5. Emit `opt/report.md`: per-pair baseline -> best cycles, speedup, commit SHA, rationale.
6. Set `phase` to `"done"`.

## Phase 5: Done

Terminal. Human reviews `opt/report.md`. Winning commits in libnn tree are the deliverable.

## State Machine

Read `opt/state.json` field `phase` to determine where to resume:

```
gather -> baseline -> iterate (loop) -> evaluate -> done
```

## State File Format

```json
{
  "mode": "single_function|model",
  "function": "conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any",
  "source_input": "Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_<func>.c",
  "source_file": "Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c",
  "macro": "ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>",
  "phase": "gather|baseline|iterate|evaluate|done",
  "iteration": 0,
  "baseline": {"cycles": 0, "instructions": 0},
  "best": {"cycles": 0, "instructions": 0, "iteration": 0, "commit": ""},
  "history": [],
  "shapes": {},
  "quant_params": {},
  "data_types": {"input": "", "weight": "", "output": ""},
  "buffer_sizes": {},
  "target_features": {}
}
```

## Key Rules

1. **Never modify `opt/config.toml`** -- user-owned configuration.
2. **Baseline code is never modified** -- optimized paths go under `#ifdef ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>`.
3. **QEMU is for correctness only** -- FPGA cycle counts are ground truth.
4. **Primary target: AX45MPV VLEN=1024** -- do not sacrifice VLEN=1024 perf.
5. **Use `NDS_VEC_*` macros only** in `ENA_VEC_ISA` blocks. Never `__riscv_*` intrinsics.
6. Macro naming: `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>` (single) or `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>_S<N>` (model).
7. Always validate config before first build (Step 0).
8. Scripts are the single source of truth for builds and tests -- never construct gcc/QEMU/GDB commands manually.

  Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
  in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
  libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
  optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
  or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
  "libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
  "phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
  opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
argument-hint: "[validate|gather|baseline|iterate|status|reset] [function_name]"

---

# libnn Optimizer

Autonomous optimization of libnn NN operator functions targeting RISC-V FPGA hardware.
Five-phase FSM: gather -> baseline -> iterate -> evaluate -> done.
State persisted in `opt/state.json`.

## Commands

- `/libnn-optimizer validate` - Run config validation (Step 0)
- `/libnn-optimizer gather <function_perf_test>` - Start Phase 1: parse task spec + target features
- `/libnn-optimizer baseline` - Run Phase 2: measure unmodified library
- `/libnn-optimizer iterate` - Run Phase 3: one optimization iteration
- `/libnn-optimizer status` - Show current state from opt/state.json
- `/libnn-optimizer reset` - Reset state to initial

Arguments received: `$ARGUMENTS`

## Project Paths

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/config.toml` | Target config (user-owned, NEVER modify) |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/state.json` | Optimization state (LLM reads/writes) |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/script/` | Validation and helper scripts |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/fpga_config/` | FPGA Verilog .inc files |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/patches/` | Per-iteration diffs (audit trail) |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/build/` | Build artifacts |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/log/` | Build/test/perf logs |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/plan.md` | Full implementation plan |
| `/home/nick/work/libnn/` | libnn source tree |

## Step 0: Validate Configuration

Run before any optimization work. Validates config.toml and all referenced resources.

```bash
python3 /home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/script/validate_config.py --strace
```

Seven sequential checks, fail-fast:

| Step | Check                                                               | On Failure                                   |
|------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------|
| 1    | Load config.toml - all required sections/keys                       | config.toml                                  |
| 2    | Toolchain - bin_dir exists, gcc with Andes flags                    | Script suggests alternatives found on system |
| 3    | Compile hello world using the above toolchain - RISC-V ELF produced | Toolchain issue                              |
| 4    | QEMU - binary exists, runs hello.adx via strace semihosting         | Check QEMU path                              |
| 5    | FPGA .inc - parse hardware features (VLEN, cache, ISA)              | Check fpga_config path                       |
| 6    | FPGA connectivity - TCP to board host:port                          | Network/board issue                          |
| 7    | FPGA run - compile + run hello on board via GDB                     | Board/GDB issue                              |

If Step 2 fails, the script auto-discovers alternative toolchain paths. Report them to the user. Do NOT modify config.toml.

If QEMU or FPGA steps fail, report the error and ask the user which steps to skip for offline development.

## Phase 1: Gather

Parse task spec and target features. No builds.

1. **Read config.toml.** Extract toolchain PATH, QEMU binary, FPGA host:port, CPU, VLEN.

2. **Read FPGA .inc file** (path from `[target].fpga_config`, relative to `opt/`). Extract:
   - Vector: `NDS_VLEN`, `NDS_DLEN`, `NDS_ELEN`
   - D-cache: `NDS_DCACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_DCACHE_WAY`
   - I-cache: `NDS_ICACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_CACHE_LINE_SIZE`
   - L2: `NDS_L2C_CACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_L2C_WAY`, `NDS_L2_DATA_WIDTH`
   - ISA: `NDS_RVV_SUPPORT`, `NDS_VECTOR_VDOT_SUPPORT`, `NDS_INT4_VECTOR_LOAD_SUPPORT`, `NDS_BFLOAT16_SUPPORT`, `NDS_QMAC_SUPPORT`
   - Bus: `NDS_BIU_DATA_WIDTH`

   Parse using regex: `` `define\s+<KEY>\s+(\d+) `` for integers, `` `define\s+<KEY>_INT\s+(\d+) `` or `` `define\s+<KEY>\s+"(yes|no)" `` for booleans.

3. **Read source_input** (perf test or model C file):
   - **Single mode:** parse `#define` block from `Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_<func>.c`. Extract shapes (INPUT_X, INPUT_Y, INPUT_CH, KERNEL_X, etc.), quantization params (OUT_SHIFT, OUT_MULT, offsets, activation min/max), buffer sizes. Resolve function name from `riscv_nn_*()` call site.
   - **Model mode:** parse model `.c` and `#include`d param headers. Build ordered `(function, shape-set)` list with call counts.

4. **Locate baseline source** at `/home/nick/work/libnn/Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c`. Record path.

5. **Derive optimization macro:** `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC_UPPER>` (single) or `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC_UPPER>_S<N>` (model).

6. **Write state.json.** Set `phase` to `"baseline"`.

**Verification:** state.json must contain:
- All shapes from the perf test `#define` block
- `target_features.vlen == 1024`
- `target_features.dcache_kb == 32`
- `target_features.l2_kb == 8192`
- Correct `source_file` path under `Source/`

## Phase 2: Baseline

Measure unmodified library on target.

1. Rebuild baseline:
   ```bash
   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/build.sh <cpu>
   ```

2. QEMU correctness (strace mode for semihosting fd 9):
   ```bash
   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh <test> --strace --vlen <vlen>
   ```
   Accept only "accuracy checking pass". Failure aborts -- baseline is broken.

3. FPGA perf measurement:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf && cd /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf
   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/test_perf.sh ax45mpv riscv64-elf-gcc BOARD <function>
   ```
   Parse `performance.csv` for cycles and instructions.

4. Write `baseline` to state.json. Initialize empty `history`. Set `phase` to `"iterate"`.

## Phase 3: Iterate

One iteration = one candidate optimization. Loop until `max_iterations` or no improvement >= `min_improvement_pct` for 2 consecutive attempts.

Per iteration:

1. **Plan** - Inspect current best source (or baseline on iter 1). Pick one concrete change. Record in `notes`.

2. **Edit** - Modify `Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c`: add optimized path under `#ifdef ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>`. Replace prior iteration's block if present. Baseline `#else` stays byte-for-byte intact.

3. **Build** - Rebuild `libnn.a` twice:
   - Without macro (sanity -- baseline still compiles)
   - With `-DENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>` for perf

4. **Correctness** - QEMU strace test. Fail -> `accepted=false`, skip perf.

5. **Perf** - `test_perf.sh`. Collect cycles/instructions.

6. **Commit** - Git commit in libnn tree. Snapshot diff to `opt/patches/iter_<N>_<func>.patch`.

7. **Accept/reject** - `accepted = pass && cycles < best.cycles`. Update `best` if accepted.

8. Append to `history`. Persist state.json.

## Phase 4: Evaluate

1. Restore `best.commit` in libnn worktree.
2. Rerun full QEMU suite for touched functions.
3. Rerun FPGA perf, confirm within +/- 0.5% of `best.cycles`.
4. Model mode only: re-profile all (function, shape-set) pairs.
5. Emit `opt/report.md`: per-pair baseline -> best cycles, speedup, commit SHA, rationale.
6. Set `phase` to `"done"`.

## Phase 5: Done

Terminal. Human reviews `opt/report.md`. Winning commits in libnn tree are the deliverable.

## State Machine

Read `opt/state.json` field `phase` to determine where to resume:

```
gather -> baseline -> iterate (loop) -> evaluate -> done
```

## State File Format

```json
{
  "mode": "single_function|model",
  "function": "conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any",
  "source_input": "Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_<func>.c",
  "source_file": "Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c",
  "macro": "ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>",
  "phase": "gather|baseline|iterate|evaluate|done",
  "iteration": 0,
  "baseline": {"cycles": 0, "instructions": 0},
  "best": {"cycles": 0, "instructions": 0, "iteration": 0, "commit": ""},
  "history": [],
  "shapes": {},
  "quant_params": {},
  "data_types": {"input": "", "weight": "", "output": ""},
  "buffer_sizes": {},
  "target_features": {}
}
```

## Key Rules

1. **Never modify `opt/config.toml`** -- user-owned configuration.
2. **Baseline code is never modified** -- optimized paths go under `#ifdef ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>`.
3. **QEMU is for correctness only** -- FPGA cycle counts are ground truth.
4. **Primary target: AX45MPV VLEN=1024** -- do not sacrifice VLEN=1024 perf.
5. **Use `NDS_VEC_*` macros only** in `ENA_VEC_ISA` blocks. Never `__riscv_*` intrinsics.
6. Macro naming: `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>` (single) or `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>_S<N>` (model).
7. Always validate config before first build (Step 0).
8. Scripts are the single source of truth for builds and tests -- never construct gcc/QEMU/GDB commands manually.

# Find a working GDB. Prefer system gdb over toolchain gdb since the
    # toolchain gdb may have mismatched libpython dependencies.
    gdb = None
    sys_gdb = shutil.which(f"{prefix}gdb")
    for candidate in [
        sys_gdb,
        os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb-nopython"),
        os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb"),
    ]:
        if candidate and os.path.isfile(candidate):
            # Quick check: can it run at all?
            r = subprocess.run([candidate, "--version"],
                               capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
            if r.returncode == 0:
                gdb = candidate
                break
    if not gdb:
        die("No working riscv64-elf-gdb found (needed for FPGA board runs)")
    ok(f"GDB: {gdb}")

    # Compile with FPGA linker script
    src = os.path.join(tmpdir, "hello_fpga.c")
    elf = os.path.join(tmpdir, "hello_fpga.adx")
    with open(src, "w") as f:
        f.write('#include <stdio.h>\nint main(void) { printf("HELLO_FPGA_OK\\n"); return 0; }\n')

---
name: libnn-optimizer
description: |
  Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
  in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
  libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
  optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
  or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
  "libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
  "phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
  opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
argument-hint: "[validate|gather|baseline|iterate|status|reset] [function_name]"
allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Edit, Write, Agent
---

# libnn Optimizer

Autonomous optimization of libnn NN operator functions targeting RISC-V FPGA hardware.
Five-phase FSM: gather -> baseline -> iterate -> evaluate -> done.
State persisted in `opt/state.json`.

## Commands

- `/libnn-optimizer validate` - Run config validation (Step 0)
- `/libnn-optimizer gather <function_perf_test>` - Start Phase 1: parse task spec + target features
- `/libnn-optimizer baseline` - Run Phase 2: measure unmodified library
- `/libnn-optimizer iterate` - Run Phase 3: one optimization iteration
- `/libnn-optimizer status` - Show current state from opt/state.json
- `/libnn-optimizer reset` - Reset state to initial

Arguments received: `$ARGUMENTS`

## Project Paths

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/config.toml` | Target config (user-owned, NEVER modify) |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/state.json` | Optimization state (LLM reads/writes) |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/script/` | Validation and helper scripts |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/fpga_config/` | FPGA Verilog .inc files |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/patches/` | Per-iteration diffs (audit trail) |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/build/` | Build artifacts |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/log/` | Build/test/perf logs |
| `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/plan.md` | Full implementation plan |
| `/home/nick/work/libnn/` | libnn source tree |

## Step 0: Validate Configuration

Run before any optimization work. Validates config.toml and all referenced resources.

```bash
python3 /home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/script/validate_config.py --strace
```

**IMPORTANT:** Run the script, print its full output to the user, and STOP. Do NOT take any further action regardless of pass or fail. The user decides next steps.

## Phase 1: Gather

Parse task spec and target features. No builds.

1. **Read config.toml.** Extract toolchain PATH, QEMU binary, FPGA host:port, CPU, VLEN.

2. **Read FPGA .inc file** (path from `[target].fpga_config`, relative to `opt/`). Extract:
   - Vector: `NDS_VLEN`, `NDS_DLEN`, `NDS_ELEN`
   - D-cache: `NDS_DCACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_DCACHE_WAY`
   - I-cache: `NDS_ICACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_CACHE_LINE_SIZE`
   - L2: `NDS_L2C_CACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_L2C_WAY`, `NDS_L2_DATA_WIDTH`
   - ISA: `NDS_RVV_SUPPORT`, `NDS_VECTOR_VDOT_SUPPORT`, `NDS_INT4_VECTOR_LOAD_SUPPORT`, `NDS_BFLOAT16_SUPPORT`, `NDS_QMAC_SUPPORT`
   - Bus: `NDS_BIU_DATA_WIDTH`

   Parse using regex: `` `define\s+<KEY>\s+(\d+) `` for integers, `` `define\s+<KEY>_INT\s+(\d+) `` or `` `define\s+<KEY>\s+"(yes|no)" `` for booleans.

3. **Read source_input** (perf test or model C file):
   - **Single mode:** parse `#define` block from `Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_<func>.c`. Extract shapes (INPUT_X, INPUT_Y, INPUT_CH, KERNEL_X, etc.), quantization params (OUT_SHIFT, OUT_MULT, offsets, activation min/max), buffer sizes. Resolve function name from `riscv_nn_*()` call site.
   - **Model mode:** parse model `.c` and `#include`d param headers. Build ordered `(function, shape-set)` list with call counts.

4. **Locate baseline source** at `/home/nick/work/libnn/Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c`. Record path.

5. **Derive optimization macro:** `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC_UPPER>` (single) or `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC_UPPER>_S<N>` (model).

6. **Write state.json.** Set `phase` to `"baseline"`.

**Verification:** state.json must contain:
- All shapes from the perf test `#define` block
- `target_features.vlen == 1024`
- `target_features.dcache_kb == 32`
- `target_features.l2_kb == 8192`
- Correct `source_file` path under `Source/`

## Phase 2: Baseline

Measure unmodified library on target.

1. Rebuild baseline:
   ```bash
   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/build.sh <cpu>
   ```

2. QEMU correctness (strace mode for semihosting fd 9):
   ```bash
   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh <test> --strace --vlen <vlen>
   ```
   Accept only "accuracy checking pass". Failure aborts -- baseline is broken.

3. FPGA perf measurement:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf && cd /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf
   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/test_perf.sh ax45mpv riscv64-elf-gcc BOARD <function>
   ```
   Parse `performance.csv` for cycles and instructions.

4. Write `baseline` to state.json. Initialize empty `history`. Set `phase` to `"iterate"`.

## Phase 3: Iterate

One iteration = one candidate optimization. Loop until `max_iterations` or no improvement >= `min_improvement_pct` for 2 consecutive attempts.

Per iteration:

1. **Plan** - Inspect current best source (or baseline on iter 1). Pick one concrete change. Record in `notes`.

2. **Edit** - Modify `Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c`: add optimized path under `#ifdef ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>`. Replace prior iteration's block if present. Baseline `#else` stays byte-for-byte intact.

3. **Build** - Rebuild `libnn.a` twice:
   - Without macro (sanity -- baseline still compiles)
   - With `-DENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>` for perf

4. **Correctness** - QEMU strace test. Fail -> `accepted=false`, skip perf.

5. **Perf** - `test_perf.sh`. Collect cycles/instructions.

6. **Commit** - Git commit in libnn tree. Snapshot diff to `opt/patches/iter_<N>_<func>.patch`.

7. **Accept/reject** - `accepted = pass && cycles < best.cycles`. Update `best` if accepted.

8. Append to `history`. Persist state.json.

## Phase 4: Evaluate

1. Restore `best.commit` in libnn worktree.
2. Rerun full QEMU suite for touched functions.
3. Rerun FPGA perf, confirm within +/- 0.5% of `best.cycles`.
4. Model mode only: re-profile all (function, shape-set) pairs.
5. Emit `opt/report.md`: per-pair baseline -> best cycles, speedup, commit SHA, rationale.
6. Set `phase` to `"done"`.

## Phase 5: Done

Terminal. Human reviews `opt/report.md`. Winning commits in libnn tree are the deliverable.

## State Machine

Read `opt/state.json` field `phase` to determine where to resume:

```
gather -> baseline -> iterate (loop) -> evaluate -> done
```

## State File Format

```json
{
  "mode": "single_function|model",
  "function": "conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any",
  "source_input": "Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_<func>.c",
  "source_file": "Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c",
  "macro": "ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>",
  "phase": "gather|baseline|iterate|evaluate|done",
  "iteration": 0,
  "baseline": {"cycles": 0, "instructions": 0},
  "best": {"cycles": 0, "instructions": 0, "iteration": 0, "commit": ""},
  "history": [],
  "shapes": {},
  "quant_params": {},
  "data_types": {"input": "", "weight": "", "output": ""},
  "buffer_sizes": {},
  "target_features": {}
}
```

## Key Rules

1. **Never modify `opt/config.toml`** -- user-owned configuration.
2. **Baseline code is never modified** -- optimized paths go under `#ifdef ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>`.
3. **QEMU is for correctness only** -- FPGA cycle counts are ground truth.
4. **Primary target: AX45MPV VLEN=1024** -- do not sacrifice VLEN=1024 perf.
5. **Use `NDS_VEC_*` macros only** in `ENA_VEC_ISA` blocks. Never `__riscv_*` intrinsics.
6. Macro naming: `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>` (single) or `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>_S<N>` (model).
7. Always validate config before first build (Step 0).
8. Scripts are the single source of truth for builds and tests -- never construct gcc/QEMU/GDB commands manually.

**IMPORTANT:** Run the script, print its full output to the user, and STOP. Do NOT take any further action regardless of pass or fail. The user decides next steps.

1	---
2	name: libnn-optimizer
3	description: |
4	  Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
5	  in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
6	  libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
7	  optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
8	  or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
9	  "libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
10	  "phase validate/gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
11	  opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
12	argument-hint: "[validate|gather|baseline|iterate|status|reset] [function_name]"
13	allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Edit, Write, Agent
14	---
15	
16	# libnn Optimizer
17	
18	Autonomous optimization of libnn NN operator functions targeting RISC-V FPGA hardware.
19	Six-phase FSM: validate -> gather -> baseline -> iterate -> evaluate -> done.
20	State persisted in `opt/state.json`.
21	
22	## Commands
23	
24	- `/libnn-optimizer validate` - Run Phase 0: config validation (pass -> gather, fail -> report)
25	- `/libnn-optimizer gather <function_perf_test>` - Run Phase 1: parse task spec + target features
26	- `/libnn-optimizer baseline` - Run Phase 2: measure unmodified library
27	- `/libnn-optimizer iterate` - Run Phase 3: one optimization iteration
28	- `/libnn-optimizer status` - Show current state from opt/state.json
29	- `/libnn-optimizer reset` - Reset state to `validate` phase
30	
31	Arguments received: `$ARGUMENTS`
32	
33	## Project Paths
34	
35	| Path | Purpose |
36	|------|---------|
37	| `$CWD/opt/config.toml` | Target config (user-owned, NEVER modify) |
38	| `$CWD/opt/state.json` | Optimization state (LLM reads/writes) |
39	| `$CWD/opt/script/` | Validation and helper scripts |
40	| `$CWD/opt/fpga_config/` | FPGA Verilog .inc files |
41	| `$CWD/opt/patches/` | Per-iteration diffs (audit trail) |
42	| `$CWD/opt/build/` | Build artifacts |
43	| `$CWD/opt/log/` | Build/test/perf logs |
44	| `$CWD/plan.md` | Full implementation plan |
45	| `/home/nick/work/libnn/` | libnn source tree |
46	
47	## Phase 0: Validate
48	
49	Initial FSM phase. Validates config.toml and all referenced resources before any optimization work.
50	
51	**Entry condition:** `phase == "validate"` (initial state) or `/libnn-optimizer validate` command.
52	
53	```bash
54	python3 $CWD/opt/script/validate_config.py --strace
55	```
56	
57	**On pass ("All steps passed"):**
58	1. Print full output to the user.
59	2. Write/update `opt/state.json` setting `phase` to `"gather"`.
60	3. Report: validation passed, ready for gather phase.
61	
62	**On fail (any step fails):**
63	1. Print full output to the user.
64	2. Keep `phase` as `"validate"` in state.json (or do not advance).
65	3. Report which steps failed. Do NOT proceed to gather.
66	
67	## Phase 1: Gather
68	
69	Parse task spec and target features. No builds.
70	
71	1. **Read config.toml.** Extract toolchain PATH, QEMU binary, FPGA host:port, CPU, VLEN.
72	
73	2. **Read FPGA .inc file** (path from `[target].fpga_config`, relative to `opt/`). Extract:
74	   - Vector: `NDS_VLEN`, `NDS_DLEN`, `NDS_ELEN`
75	   - D-cache: `NDS_DCACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_DCACHE_WAY`
76	   - I-cache: `NDS_ICACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_CACHE_LINE_SIZE`
77	   - L2: `NDS_L2C_CACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_L2C_WAY`, `NDS_L2_DATA_WIDTH`
78	   - ISA: `NDS_RVV_SUPPORT`, `NDS_VECTOR_VDOT_SUPPORT`, `NDS_INT4_VECTOR_LOAD_SUPPORT`, `NDS_BFLOAT16_SUPPORT`, `NDS_QMAC_SUPPORT`
79	   - Bus: `NDS_BIU_DATA_WIDTH`
80	
81	   Parse using regex: `` `define\s+<KEY>\s+(\d+) `` for integers, `` `define\s+<KEY>_INT\s+(\d+) `` or `` `define\s+<KEY>\s+"(yes|no)" `` for booleans.
82	
83	3. **Read source_input** (perf test C file):
84	   Parse `#define` block from `Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_<func>.c`. Extract shapes (INPUT_X, INPUT_Y, INPUT_CH, KERNEL_X, etc.), quantization params (OUT_SHIFT, OUT_MULT, offsets, activation min/max), buffer sizes. Resolve function name from `riscv_nn_*()` call site.
85	
86	4. **Locate baseline source** at `/home/nick/work/libnn/Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c`. Record path.
87	
88	5. **Derive optimization macro:** `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC_UPPER>`.
89	
90	6. **Write state.json.** Set `phase` to `"baseline"`.
91	
92	**Verification:** state.json must contain:
93	- All shapes from the perf test `#define` block
94	- `target_features.vlen == 1024`
95	- `target_features.dcache_kb == 32`
96	- `target_features.l2_kb == 8192`
97	- Correct `source_file` path under `Source/`
98	
99	## Phase 2: Baseline
100	
101	Measure unmodified library on target.
102	
103	1. Rebuild baseline:
104	   ```bash
105	   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/build.sh <cpu>
106	   ```
107	
108	2. QEMU correctness (strace mode for semihosting fd 9):
109	   ```bash
110	   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh <test> --strace --vlen <vlen>
111	   ```
112	   Accept only "accuracy checking pass". Failure aborts -- baseline is broken.
113	
114	3. FPGA perf measurement:
115	   ```bash
116	   mkdir -p /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf && cd /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf
117	   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/test_perf.sh ax45mpv riscv64-elf-gcc BOARD <function>
118	   ```
119	   Parse `performance.csv` for cycles and instructions.
120	
121	4. Write `baseline` to state.json. Initialize empty `history`. Set `phase` to `"iterate"`.
122	
123	## Phase 3: Iterate
124	
125	One iteration = one candidate optimization. Loop until `max_iterations` or no improvement >= `min_improvement_pct` for 2 consecutive attempts.
126	
127	Per iteration:
128	
129	1. **Plan** - Inspect current best source (or baseline on iter 1). Pick one concrete change. Record in `notes`.
130	
131	2. **Edit** - Modify `Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c`: add optimized path under `#ifdef ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>`. Replace prior iteration's block if present. Baseline `#else` stays byte-for-byte intact.
132	
133	3. **Build** - Rebuild `libnn.a` twice:
134	   - Without macro (sanity -- baseline still compiles)
135	   - With `-DENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>` for perf
136	
137	4. **Correctness** - QEMU strace test. Fail -> `accepted=false`, skip perf.
138	
139	5. **Perf** - `test_perf.sh`. Collect cycles/instructions.
140	
141	6. **Commit** - Git commit in libnn tree. Snapshot diff to `opt/patches/iter_<N>_<func>.patch`.
142	
143	7. **Accept/reject** - `accepted = pass && cycles < best.cycles`. Update `best` if accepted.
144	
145	8. Append to `history`. Persist state.json.
146	
147	## Phase 4: Evaluate
148	
149	1. Restore `best.commit` in libnn worktree.
150	2. Rerun full QEMU suite for touched functions.
151	3. Rerun FPGA perf, confirm within +/- 0.5% of `best.cycles`.
152	4. Emit `opt/report.md`: baseline -> best cycles, speedup, commit SHA, rationale.
153	6. Set `phase` to `"done"`.
154	
155	## Phase 5: Done
156	
157	Terminal. Human reviews `opt/report.md`. Winning commits in libnn tree are the deliverable.
158	
159	## State Machine
160	
161	Read `opt/state.json` field `phase` to determine where to resume:
162	
163	```
164	validate -> gather -> baseline -> iterate (loop) -> evaluate -> done
165	```
166	
167	## State File Format
168	
169	```json
170	{
171	  "mode": "single_function",
172	  "function": "conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any",
173	  "source_input": "Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_<func>.c",
174	  "source_file": "Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c",
175	  "macro": "ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>",
176	  "phase": "validate|gather|baseline|iterate|evaluate|done",
177	  "iteration": 0,
178	  "baseline": {"cycles": 0, "instructions": 0},
179	  "best": {"cycles": 0, "instructions": 0, "iteration": 0, "commit": ""},
180	  "history": [],
181	  "shapes": {},
182	  "quant_params": {},
183	  "data_types": {"input": "", "weight": "", "output": ""},
184	  "buffer_sizes": {},
185	  "target_features": {}
186	}
187	```
188	
189	## Key Rules
190	
191	1. **Never modify `opt/config.toml`** -- user-owned configuration.
192	2. **Baseline code is never modified** -- optimized paths go under `#ifdef ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>`.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- algorithmic-art: Creating algorithmic art using p5.js with seeded randomness and interactive parameter exploration. Use this when users request creating art using code, generative art, algorithmic art, flow fields, or particle systems. Create original algorithmic art rather than copying existing artists' work to avoid copyright violations.
- brand-guidelines: Applies Anthropic's official brand colors and typography to any sort of artifact that may benefit from having Anthropic's look-and-feel. Use it when brand colors or style guidelines, visual formatting, or company design standards apply.
- canvas-design: Create beautiful visual art in .png and .pdf documents using design philosophy. You should use this skill when the user asks to create a poster, piece of art, design, or other static piece. Create original visual designs, never copying existing artists' work to avoid copyright violations.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. TRIGGER when: code imports anthropic/@anthropic-ai/sdk; user asks to use the Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Managed Agents (/v1/agents, /v1/sessions, /v1/environments). DO NOT TRIGGER when: code imports `openai`/other AI SDK, general programming, or ML/data-science tasks.
- doc-coauthoring: Guide users through a structured workflow for co-authoring documentation. Use when user wants to write documentation, proposals, technical specs, decision docs, or similar structured content. This workflow helps users efficiently transfer context, refine content through iteration, and verify the doc works for readers. Trigger when user mentions writing docs, creating proposals, drafting specs, or similar documentation tasks.
- docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- internal-comms: A set of resources to help me write all kinds of internal communications, using the formats that my company likes to use. Claude should use this skill whenever asked to write some sort of internal communications (status reports, leadership updates, 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQs, incident reports, project updates, etc.).
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase validate/gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- slack-gif-creator: Knowledge and utilities for creating animated GIFs optimized for Slack. Provides constraints, validation tools, and animation concepts. Use when users request animated GIFs for Slack like "make me a GIF of X doing Y for Slack."
- theme-factory: Toolkit for styling artifacts with a theme. These artifacts can be slides, docs, reportings, HTML landing pages, etc. There are 10 pre-set themes with colors/fonts that you can apply to any artifact that has been creating, or can generate a new theme on-the-fly.
- web-artifacts-builder: Suite of tools for creating elaborate, multi-component claude.ai HTML artifacts using modern frontend web technologies (React, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui). Use for complex artifacts requiring state management, routing, or shadcn/ui components - not for simple single-file HTML/JSX artifacts.
- webapp-testing: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser logs.
- xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- statusline: Set up Claude Code's status line UI
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
- insights: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions
- team-onboarding: Help teammates ramp on Claude Code with a guide from your usage
